steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize the significance of a similarity between two kits. The notes show that CTAB 2X failed on purity (ratio 2.2, far from the ideal 1.8) and Pplant failed on yield (12.5 ng/μL is too low). The researchers concluded Pbact was the only effective kit. The significant shared trait between CTAB 2X and Pplant is that both fell short — each for a different criterion — and were therefore deemed less effective.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘CTAB 2X and Pplant both had a DNA yield above 10 and a purity ratio above 1.4.’: This lists raw numbers without any significance attached. It does not explain why the similarity matters. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Due to their insufficient yield or purity, CTAB 2X and Pplant were deemed by the researchers to be less effective than Pbact.’: This names the shared outcome (both deemed less effective) and its significance (insufficient yield or purity), drawing directly on the researchers’ stated conclusion. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘With the ideal purity ratio being 1.8, CTAB 2X and Pbact were equal in purity, according to the researchers.’: This compares the wrong pair — CTAB 2X and Pbact, not CTAB 2X and Pplant. Also, CTAB 2X had a ratio of 2.2 and Pbact had 1.6; they are not equal in purity. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Compared to CTAB 2X, which had a DNA yield of 2,134 ng/μL, both Pbact and Pplant had insufficient yields; Pplant, in particular, was ineffective due to its low yield.’: This groups Pbact with Pplant as having insufficient yields, but the notes say Pbact’s yield was sufficiently high — that is why it was chosen. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
